FR-001.title: Implement instructions fold each task's finish into the shared context the moment it completes (per-task, foreground), not once per wave

FR-002.title: Parallel workers stay append-only; only the main agent folds — one writer of the shared files

FR-003.title: Plan and tasks bodies record a script-stamped extension start at the beginning of their work

FR-004.title: After-plan/after-tasks hooks record the step's completion (extension), not a start

FR-005.title: Timing part drops AI step-level self-close for plan/tasks; clarify/analyze keep it; substeps unchanged

FR-006.title: GUI dispatch preamble mirrors the per-task cadence and the new closure model

FR-007.title: Derivation trusts new-order runs (4 of 4) and keeps legacy inverted spans untrusted, with tests
tests: src/features/specs/__tests__/stepHistoryDerivation.test.ts::trusts all four phases for the fixed capture order

FR-008.title: Assembled bodies, goldens, and shape-parity gate agree after the change

FR-009.title: Capture docs describe the new cadence and boundary-ownership model

context: constraint: assembled bodies are generated — edit parts/nodes, re-bless goldens deliberately
constraint: forward-only status guard and idempotent completion appends must hold
intent: Make Companion timing capture honest: per-task live progress folded by the main agent, and extension-stamped step boundaries in order so all four phases carry trusted durations
expectations: No change to stock-family timing trust (stays best-effort)
No retroactive trusting of legacy inverted-order data
approach: Move the implement fold from per-wave to per-task (main agent, foreground; workers stay append-only) and make plan/tasks boundaries extension-stamped in order: bodies record a script-stamped start, after-step hooks record the complete, and the AI step-level self-close for plan/tasks is removed. No writer or derivation code changes; proven by derivation tests, re-blessed goldens, and shape parity.

decisions.decision: Approach (b): extension stamps both step boundaries (body start + after-hook complete)
decisions.why: The command body is the one surface every dispatch path shares and the hook reliably runs at the true step end; GUI-dispatch stamping (a) misses the skill/auto/workflow paths that produced the 484 data
decisions.rejected: Approach (a) dispatch-time stamping; trusting by:ai closes in derivation

decisions.decision: No deriveStepHistory changes — prove 4-of-4 with tests only
decisions.why: Correctly ordered boundaries already satisfy the existing trust rule; changing close-preference would rewrite historical spans
decisions.rejected: Preferring own completion over next-step boundary for completedAt

decisions.decision: Per-task materialize by the MAIN agent only; workers append-only
decisions.why: materialize is a read-modify-write of shared files — one named serializing writer, per the concurrency rule; idempotent fold makes per-task + wave-join backstop safe
decisions.rejected: Workers materializing their own finishes; extension-side sidecar watcher

plan.summary: Prompt-surface-only fix: per-task fold cadence + extension-stamped plan/tasks boundaries via body starts and hook completes
plan.key_finding: The completion-append idempotency race is why the AI self-close must be removed — an earlier by:ai complete would block the hook by:extension complete forever

tasks.summary: 12 tasks across 4 waves: boundary fixes (4 parallel) -> timing-part + tests -> cadence rewrites -> preamble mirror -> regenerate/docs/validate

implement.summary: Per-task fold cadence + ordered extension-stamped plan/tasks boundaries shipped across timing part, nodes, hook commands, GUI preamble, docs; all gates green

T001.did: Plan body now records a script-stamped extension start as its opening action

T002.did: Tasks body now records a script-stamped extension start as its opening action

T003.did: after-plan hook now records the extension-stamped completion boundary (--kind complete)

T004.did: after-tasks hook now records the extension-stamped completion boundary (--kind complete)

T005.did: Timing part self-close narrowed to clarify/analyze; names the idempotency race that forces removing plan/tasks self-close

T006.did: Added fixed-order derivation test: 4-of-4 trusted phases with complete timing summary; legacy inverted order stays covered by the 484 model

T007.did: Timing part fold cadence is now per-task by the MAIN agent (foreground, one at a time); wave-join fold demoted to backstop; workers stay append-only

T008.did: implement-exec closes a task with append+materialize back to back by the MAIN agent; wave join keeps reconcile plus backstop fold

T009.did: GUI preambles name the main agent as the per-task serializing writer; slim body reflects hook-stamped plan/tasks closes

T010.did: Regenerated 13 assembled bodies, re-blessed 14 goldens; build/assemble/shape-parity gates all pass; tracked stock skills carry no timing part so no emission refresh needed

T011.did: capture-and-timing doc gained the #509 section and cadence/boundary rewrites; template-profiles, spec-kit commands doc, and spec-kit CHANGELOG updated

T012.did: compile clean; 123 suites / 1724 jest tests pass; all python suites pass; manifest + emissions gates OK

verified.what: Full jest suite incl. new 4-of-4 trusted-phases derivation test

verified.result: 14 bodies match parts, golden, timing fence

verified.what: Live proof on this very spec
verified.result: tasks.md checkboxes advanced one task at a time; plan/tasks captured start-before-complete
